$comment: Catalog of managed AWS Frontier Agents deployable from the control plane. Each entry drives the deploy form on the UI and the packaging step on the backend. Keep ids stable — they are used as the template_id suffix in deployment records.

agents.name: AWS DevOps Agent
agents.description: Amazon's managed DevOps Agent — monitors infrastructure and deployments, investigates incidents, and recommends remediations across single or multiple AWS accounts.

parameters.name: agent_space_name
parameters.label: Agent Space Name
parameters.description: Name assigned to the DevOps Agent Space.

parameters.name: agent_space_description
parameters.label: Agent Space Description
parameters.default: DevOps Agent Space provisioned by AVA
parameters.description: Free-text description shown on the Agent Space.

parameters.name: name_postfix
parameters.label: Resource Name Postfix
parameters.description: Fixed suffix appended to IAM role names. Leave empty to use a random 4-byte hex on first apply. Useful for re-deploys that need stable role names.

agents.$comment_advanced_parameters: Cross-account monitoring (Part 2 of the Terraform module) is not wired through the CI/CD pipeline yet — the shared CodeBuild role has no path to assume the secondary account. The advanced parameters below are preserved here for when the pipeline gains a cross-account provider alias. Restore them into advanced_parameters[] to re-expose the collapsible section in the deploy form.

$disabled_advanced_parameters.name: service_account_id
$disabled_advanced_parameters.label: Secondary Account ID (optional)
$disabled_advanced_parameters.description: Account ID of a secondary (service) account to monitor. Requires a follow-up apply after Part 1 outputs the Agent Space ARN, with the aws.service provider alias configured. Leave empty to skip cross-account setup.

$disabled_advanced_parameters.name: agent_space_arn
$disabled_advanced_parameters.label: Agent Space ARN (optional)
$disabled_advanced_parameters.description: ARN emitted by an earlier Part 1 apply. Only set this when enabling cross-account monitoring in Part 2.

agents.name: AWS Security Agent
agents.description: Amazon's managed Security Agent — proactive application security across the development lifecycle. Provisions an Agent Space and three service-scoped IAM roles (Application, Penetration Test Service, Actor) for design review, code review, and on-demand pentesting.

parameters.name: agent_space_name
parameters.label: Agent Space Name
parameters.description: Name assigned to the Security Agent Space.

parameters.name: agent_space_description
parameters.label: Agent Space Description
parameters.default: Security Agent Space provisioned by AVA
parameters.description: Free-text description shown on the Agent Space.

parameters.name: name_postfix
parameters.label: Resource Name Postfix
parameters.description: Fixed suffix appended to IAM role names. Leave empty to use a random 4-byte hex on first apply. Useful for re-deploys that need stable role names.

parameters.name: external_id
parameters.label: External ID
parameters.description: External ID required when assuming the PenTest Service Role and Actor Role. Leave empty to auto-generate (Terraform) or derive from the stack ID (CloudFormation). Pin a value for production.

agents.$comment_auto_injected: create_application and existing_application_domain are auto-detected by the deploy route handler (api/routes/frontier_agents.py). The backend queries AWS::SecurityAgent::Application via Cloud Control, sets create_application based on existence, and looks up the domain when reusing. Hidden from the UI on purpose so users don't have to know about the singleton constraint.
